chunks.text: A modern corporation spends a great amount on advertising in order to make its brand known to as many people as possible.
Through media such as television, newspapers, magazines, and the internet
countless advertisement reach us every day.
The purpose of an advertisement is to attract the attention of the consumer and persuade them toward a purchase.
The image of a brand, just as much as the quality of a product
Since the appearance of social media
consumers themselves have been able to broadcast their opinion to the world.
An opinion against a certain brand can spread across the internet within hours
and the image of a corporation may shift overnight.
Governments, too, have set up several regulation to protect consumers from misleading advertising.
For example, products related to health may not make any claim without solid evidence.
Today's consumption society binds corporations, consumers
and the state in a triangular relationship in which each watches the others.
We ourselves are required to keep a critical eye on what we are shown.
